candidates.evidence_quote: The Cheesecake Factory's comparable sales were negative 2.2% in the fourth quarter, down from 0.3% in the third quarter
Industry sales decelerated in the fourth quarter as reflected by the black box casual dining index declining sequentially by 410 basis points from the third quarter
we believe is about a 1% net impact, negative net impact on the entire quarter... We did see, you know, some record closures. I think we probably had 120 restaurants closed on the peak day

cost of sales decreased 70 basis points, with 40 basis points attributable to the gift card breakage benefit to revenue, with the remainder primarily driven by favorable commodity costs and mix shift partially offset by higher beef costs
while beef is higher, it's not as big of a piece for us. And dairy is measurably lower. So our market basket, I think, is aiding us in that endeavor
dairy is measurably lower. So our market basket, I think, is aiding us in that endeavor

Labor, as a percent of sales, declined 40 basis points... We are modeling net total labor inflation of low to mid single digits when factoring in the latest trends in wage rates and minimum wage increases, as well as other components of labor
the continued improvement and retention, supporting labor productivity gains and wage leverage... we've had tremendous retention in the restaurants. We've also had tremendous retention at corporate
The remaining difference was primarily driven by higher group medical expenses, partially offset by the continued improvement and retention
Adjusted restaurant level profit margins at the Cheesecake Factory increased 60 basis points year over year to 17.6%, with margin expansion also realized at North Italia and Flower Child
Q4 pricing was about 3.5% to 4%... with this year, Cheesecake will be about 3%. So we're bringing that down
mix was a negative 1.8... we do think that the negative mix will continue, but at a lesser rate as we progress through the year
supported by an off-premise sales mix of 22%, a slight improvement from recent quarters

Culinary innovation remains a core strength and an important differentiator for our business. The new menu items we introduced across a wide range of categories and price points continue to resonate well with guests
Results have been encouraging, with year-over-year growth in appetizer attachment rates and improved entree ordering patterns
we finished the year with 25 new openings, delivering approximately 7% unit growth for 2025. Looking ahead, we expect to open as many as 26 restaurants in 2026

North Italia fourth quarter annualized AUVs totaled $7.6 million. Comparable sales declined 4%, reflecting broader industry sales trends, continued pressure from sales transfer related to recently opened restaurants, as well as the lingering impact of the Los Angeles fires
sales transfer related to recently opened restaurants... we're seeing evidence that there was truly the cannibalization and the fire as the comp is recovering
the lingering impact of the Los Angeles fires... we're seeing evidence that there was truly the cannibalization and the fire as the comp is recovering
Flower Child continued to perform exceptionally well and meaningfully outpaced the Fast Casual segment. Fourth quarter comparable sales increased 4%, for a two-year comp sales increase of 15%
we anticipate total revenues for fiscal 2026 to be approximately $3.9 billion at the midpoint of our sensitivity modeling
adjusted diluted earnings per share was $1, both finishing toward the higher end of our expectations
many factors in terms of whether you want to believe it's the K economy or the government shutdown. And we have a lot of historical data to understand those trends
highlighted by strong membership growth and improved engagement... we expect to launch a dedicated rewards app in the coming months
Total revenues were $961.6 million, inclusive of $17.3 million of gift card breakage revenue as a result of a change in historical redemption patterns

we announced an increase to our share repurchase authorization and raised our quarterly dividend for the first quarter... we returned $24 million to our shareholders in the form of dividends and stock repurchases
Q4 pricing was about 3.5% to 4% and mix was a negative 1.8 and then traffic was the delta from that
We are modeling net total labor inflation of low to mid single digits when factoring in the latest trends in wage rates and minimum wage increases, as well as other components of labor
We see a nice little comeback in bar incident rates at North, which is good. It's an important part of the concept and Bar mix is about 23% have been very, very stable
Adjusted annualized AUVs were $12.2 million for the quarter, supported by an off-premise sales mix of 22%
working a little bit on that lunch day part because that was where we felt a little bit of the pressure over the past few quarters
